elements.sent-2411-001: We introduce the concept of generalized almost plastic structure, and, on a pseudo-Riemannian manifold endowed with two $(1,1)$-tensor fields satisfying some compatibility conditions, we construct a family of generalized almost plastic structures and characterize their integrability with respect to a given affine connection on the manifold.
elements.sent-2411-002: Let $M$ be a smooth manifold and let $E$ be a fiber bundle over $M$.
elements.sent-2411-003: Let us denote by $\Gamma ^\infty (E)$ the set of smooth sections of $E$.
elements.sent-2411-004: A polynomial structure on $E$ is a morphism $J:\Gamma ^\infty (E) \rightarrow \Gamma ^\infty (E)$ such that there exists a polynomial $P$ with $P(J)=0$.
elements.sent-2411-005: Almost complex structures, almost product structures, and metallic structures are examples of polynomial structures.
elements.sent-2411-006: A morphism $J$ such that $J^3-pJ-qI=0$, where $p,q$ are positive integers and $I$ is the identity operator on $\Gamma ^\infty (E)$, is called an almost nylon structure, and, if $p=q=1$, then, it is called an almost plastic structure.
elements.sent-2411-007: The name plastic goes back to Hans van der Laan (1924) and G.
elements.sent-2411-008: Cordonnier (1928) who discovered architectural proportions based on the unique real solution of the cubic equation $x^3-x-1=0$, given by $\rho=\sqrt[3]{\frac{9+\sqrt {69 {18 +\sqrt[3]{{\frac{9-\sqrt {69 {18 }$ and called it, the plastic number, \cite{m .}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-009: The present paper aims to focus on almost plastic structures on the generalized tangent bundle $E=TM\oplus T^*M$.
elements.sent-2411-010: In this spirit, we will define a generalized almost plastic structure on $M$ as being an almost plastic structure on $E$.
elements.sent-2411-011: Given a pseudo-Riemannian metric on $M$, we construct a family of generalized almost plastic structures induced by two $(1,1)$-tensor fields on $M$ satisfying some compatibility conditions, and we characterize their integrability with respect to a given affine connection on $M$.
elements.sent-2411-012: On the other hand, we define a generalized almost plastic structure by means of a $(1,1)$-tensor field on $M$ satisfying the cubic equation $x^3-x+1=0$ and we establish sufficient integrability conditions in terms of quasi-statistical structures.
elements.sent-2411-013: We remark that this construction gives rise to a {\bf{duality between the two equations $x^3-x-1=0$ and $x^3-x+1=0$ in terms of associated generalized structures.}}
elements.sent-2411-014: Let $\mathbb R (n)$ be the set of real square matrices of order $n$ and let $A\in \mathbb R (n)$.
elements.sent-2411-015: Then, $A$ is called a plastic matrix if $A$ satisfies the equation: \begin{equation}A^3-A-I=0,\end{equation} where $I$ is the identity matrix.
elements.sent-2411-016: We determine the expressions of the plastic matrices of order $2$, as follows.
elements.sent-2411-017: Let $A \in \mathbb R (2)$ be a plastic matrix.
elements.sent-2411-018: Then, $A$ has one of the following forms: \begin{equation}\label{M10}A=\rho I,\end{equation} where $\rho$ is the plastic number and $I$ is the identity matrix, or \begin{equation}\label{M20}A=\begin{pmatrix} a_{11} \displaystyle {\frac {1- a_{11} a_ {22}- a_{11}^2- a_{22}^2}{ a_{21}}} a_{21} a_{22} \end{pmatrix}\end{equation} with $a_{11 , a_{22}\in \mathbb R$, $ a_{21 \in \mathbb R\setminus \{0\}$ and $(trA)^3-trA+1=0$, where $trA:=a_{11 +a_{22}$.}}}
elements.formula-2411-003: \begin{equation}\label{M20}A= a_{11} \displaystyle {\frac {1- a_{11} a_ {22}- a_{11}^2- a_{22}^2}{ a_{21}}} a_{21} a_{22} \end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-021: If we suppose $a_{21 =0$ (respectively, $a_{12 =0$), then we get that $a_{11 =a_{22}$ is the plastic number and furthermore, also that $a_{12 =0$ (respectively, also that $a_{21 =0$), hence, we have (\ref{M10 ).}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-023: In particular, we get: \begin{equation}\left \{ {l} a_{12}a_{21}=1-a_{11}a_{22}- a_{11}^2-a_{22}^2 (a_{11}+a_{22})^3-(a_{11}+a_{22})+1=0  \right. ,\end{equation} hence, we have (\ref{M20 ), and the proof is complete.}}
elements.sent-2411-024: Then, $A=\rho I$, where $\rho$ is the plastic number and $I$ is the identity matrix, or there exists an invertible matrix $C \in \mathbb R (2)$, such that $A=C^{-1 BC$, where \begin{equation}\label{M30}B=\begin{pmatrix} \alpha 1-\alpha^2 1 0 \end{pmatrix},\end{equation} for $\alpha$ the unique real solution of the equation $x^3-x+1=0$.}
elements.sent-2411-025: If $A$ has a real eigenvalue, then, it has multiplicity two and $A=\rho I$.
elements.sent-2411-026: If $A$ has not real eigenvalues, then, it has two complex conjugate eigenvalues and, as a consequence of the previous proposition, their sum is the real solution, $\alpha$, of the equation $x^3-x+1=0$.
elements.sent-2411-027: In particular, if \begin{equation}A=\begin{pmatrix} a_{11} \displaystyle {\frac {1- a_{11} a_ {22}- a_{11}^2- a_{22}^2}{ a_{21}}} a_{21} a_{22} \end{pmatrix},\end{equation} then we can choose \begin{equation}C= a_{21} a_ {22} 0 1 \end{equation} and, as $\alpha=a_{11 +a_{22}$, we get the statement.}
elements.formula-2411-010: \begin{equation}A= a_{11} \displaystyle {\frac {1- a_{11} a_ {22}- a_{11}^2- a_{22}^2}{ a_{21}}} a_{21} a_{22} ,\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-029: An almost plastic structure on $M$ is a tensor field $J$, of type $(1,1)$, on $M$, which satisfies the equation: \begin{equation}J^3-J-I=0.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-030: If $J$ is an almost plastic structure on $M$, then, $(M,J)$ is called an almost plastic manifold.
elements.sent-2411-032: An almost plastic pseudo-Riemannian structure on $(M,g)$ is an almost plastic structure $J$ on $M$ such that $g(JX,Y)=g(X,JY)$ for all $X,Y\in\Gamma^\infty(TM)$.
elements.sent-2411-033: If $J$ is an almost plastic pseudo-Riemannian structure on $(M,g)$, then, $(M,g,J)$ is called an almost plastic pseudo-Rieman\-ni\-an manifold.
elements.sent-2411-034: Let $N(J)$ be the Nijenhuis tensor field of $J$, defined as: \begin{equation}N(J)(X,Y):=[JX,JY]-J[JX,Y]-J[X,JY]+J^2[X,Y]\end{equation} for all $X,Y\in\Gamma^\infty(TM)$.
elements.sent-2411-035: According to \cite{v , we say that an almost plastic structure $J$ is integrable if $N(J)=0$.}
elements.sent-2411-036: We will call plastic structure an integrable almost plastic structure.
elements.sent-2411-037: An almost plastic manifold $(M,J)$ with $J$ integrable will be called a plastic manifold.
elements.sent-2411-039: Then, $J=\rho I$ is a pseudo-Riemannian plastic structure on any pseudo-Riemannian manifold $(M,g)$ which is called the \emph {trivial pseudo-Riemannian plastic structure .}
elements.sent-2411-040: We remark that the concept of "almost plastic Riemannian structure" has no sense because, excepting the trivial case $J=\rho I$, a plastic structure is not real diagonalizable and then it cannot be symmetric with respect to a positive definite scalar product.
elements.sent-2411-041: We remark that an almost plastic structure $J$ is invertible and the inverse tensor field is given by $J^{-1 =J^2-I$.. Indeed, $J(J^2-I)=J^3-J=I$.}
elements.sent-2411-042: However, $J^{-1}$ is not an almost plastic structure because $(J^{-1 )^3-J^{-1 -I=-J$.}}
elements.sent-2411-043: A metallic structure on $M$, \cite{metalic , is a tensor field $J$, of type $(1,1)$, on $M$, which satisfies the equation: \begin{equation}J^2-pJ-qI=0,\end{equation} where $p,q$ are positive integers.}
elements.sent-2411-044: We remark that, if $J$ is a metallic structure, then, $J$ is an almost plastic structure if and only if \begin{equation}\left \{ {l} p^3-p+1=0 q=1-p^2  \right. \ \ or \ \ \left \{ {l} q\neq 1-p^2 J=\displaystyle{\frac{1-pq}{p^2+q-1}}I  \right. ,\end{equation} respectively, $J$ satisfies a dual equation, $J^3-J+I=0$, if and only if \begin{equation}\left \{ {l} p=\rho \ \ is the plastic number q=1-\rho^2  \right. \ \ or \ \ \left \{ {l} q\neq 1-p^2 J=-\displaystyle{\frac{1+pq}{p^2+q-1}}I  \right. .\end{equation}.}}}}
elements.formula-2411-016: \begin{equation}\left \{ {l} p=\rho \ \ is the plastic number q=1-\rho^2  \right. \ \ or \ \ \left \{ {l} q\neq 1-p^2 J=-\displaystyle{\frac{1+pq}{p^2+q-1}}I  \right. .}}\end{equation}
elements.subsection-2411-003: Geometrical properties of the generalized tangent bundle
elements.sent-2411-045: Let $TM\oplus T^*M$ be the generalized tangent bundle of $M$.
elements.sent-2411-046: On $TM\oplus T^*M$, we consider the natural indefinite metric \begin{equation}<X+\eta,Y+\beta>:=-\frac{1}{2}(\eta(Y)+\beta(X))\end{equation} and the natural symplectic structure \begin{equation}(X+\eta,Y+\beta):=-\frac{1}{2}(\eta(Y)-\beta(X))\end{equation} for all $X,Y\in \Gamma^{\infty (TM)$ and $\eta,\beta\in \Gamma^\infty(T^*M).$ If $g$ is a non-degenerate $(0,2)$-tensor field on $M$, we will denote by $g$ the flat isomorphism $\flat_g:\Gamma^{\infty (TM)\rightarrow \Gamma^{\infty (T^*M)$, $\flat_g(X):=i_Xg$, and by $g^{-1}$ its inverse, and we define the bilinear form, $\check g$, on $TM\oplus T^*M$ by: \begin{equation}\check g(X+\eta, Y+\beta):=g(X,Y)+g(g^{-1}(\eta),g^{-1}(\beta))\end{equation} for all $X,Y\in \Gamma^\infty(TM)$ and $\eta,\beta\in \Gamma^\infty(T^*M)$.}}}

elements.sent-2411-050: Any morphism $\hat J:\Gamma^{\infty (TM\oplus T^*M)\rightarrow \Gamma^{\infty (TM\oplus T^*M)$ will be called a generalized structure on $M$.}}
elements.sent-2411-051: Let $\hat J:\Gamma^{\infty (TM\oplus T^*M)\rightarrow \Gamma^{\infty (TM\oplus T^*M)$ be a generalized structure on $M$.}}
elements.sent-2411-052: If $\hat J$ satisfies the equation \begin{equation}{\hat J}^3-\hat J-I=0,\end{equation} then, $\hat J$ is called a generalized almost plastic structure on $M$.
elements.sent-2411-053: Then, the generalized structure \begin{equation}\label{m10}\hat{J}:=\begin{pmatrix} J_1 0 0 J_2^* \ \end{pmatrix},\end{equation} where $J_2^*:\Gamma^ \infty(T^*M)\rightarrow \Gamma^ \infty (T^*M)$ is defined by $J_2^*(\eta)(X):=\eta(J_2X)$ for all $\eta \in \Gamma^ \infty(T^*M)$ and $X\in \Gamma ^\infty (TM)$, is a generalized almost plastic structure on $M$.
elements.sent-2411-054: For $J_1=J_2=J$, we will denote the induced generalized almost plastic structure by: \begin{equation}\label{M100}\hat{J}:=\begin{pmatrix} J 0 0 J^* \end{pmatrix}.\end{equation}. \end{example.}
elements.sent-2411-055: Let $\hat J$ be the generalized almost plastic structure defined by (\ref{M100 ).}
elements.sent-2411-056: Then, \begin{equation}<\hat J(X+\eta),Y+\beta>=<X+\eta,\hat J(Y+\beta)>;\end{equation} moreover, if $g$ is a pseudo-Riemannian metric and $(M,g,J_1), (M,g,J_2)$ are almost plastic pseudo-Riemannian manifolds, then, the structure ${\hat J}$ defined by (\ref{m10 ) satisfies: \begin{equation}\check g(\hat J(X+\eta), Y+\beta)=\check g(X+\eta,\hat J(Y+\beta))\end{equation} for all $X,Y\in \Gamma^\infty(TM)$ and $\eta,\beta\in \Gamma^\infty(T^*M)$.}
elements.sent-2411-057: We have: \begin{equation}<\hat J(X+\eta), Y+\beta>=<JX+J^*(\eta),Y+\beta>=-\frac{1}{2}(\eta(JY)+\beta(JX));\end{equation} on the other hand, \begin{equation}<X+\eta, \hat J(Y+\beta)>=<X+\eta,JY+J^*(\beta)>=-\frac{1}{2}(\eta(JY)+\beta(JX)),\end{equation} and the first statement is proved.
elements.sent-2411-058: Moreover, ${\hat J}$ defined by (\ref{m10 ) satisfies: \begin{equation}\check g({\hat J}(X+\eta), Y+\beta) =\check g(J_1X+J_2^*(\eta),Y+\beta)=g(J_1X,Y)+g(g^{-1}(J_2^*(\eta)),g^{-1}(\beta)) =g(X,J_1Y)+g(J_2(g^{-1}(\eta)),g^{-1}(\beta)) =g(X,J_1Y)+g(g^{-1}(\eta),J_2(g^{-1}(\beta))) =g(X,J_1Y)+g(g^{-1}(\eta),g^{-1}(J_2^*(\beta))) =\check g(X+\eta,{\hat J}(Y+\beta)),\end{equation} and the proof is complete.}

elements.subsection-2411-005: Generalized almost plastic structures defined by two $(1,1)$-tensor fields
elements.sent-2411-064: Taking inspiration from the form of order two plastic matrices, we now construct a family of generalized almost plastic structures on a pseudo-Riemannian manifold.
elements.sent-2411-065: Let $(M,g)$ be a pseudo-Riemannian manifold and let $J_1$, $J_2$ be two $(1,1)$-tensor fields on $M$ satisfying the following conditions: (i) $ J_1J_2=J_2J_1$ (ii) $(J_1+J_2)^3-(J_1+J_2)+I=0$ (iii) $g(J_1X,Y)=g(X,J_1Y)$, for all $X,Y\in \Gamma^\infty(TM)$ (iv) $g(J_2X,Y)=g(X,J_2Y)$, for all $X,Y\in \Gamma^\infty(TM)$.
elements.sent-2411-066: Then, \begin{equation}\label{m15}\hat{J}:=\begin{pmatrix} J_1 (I-J_1J_2-J^2_1-J^2_2)g^{-1} g J_2^* \end{pmatrix}\end{equation} is a generalized almost plastic structure on $M$.
elements.sent-2411-067: From \begin{equation}\hat J(X+\eta)=\left(J_1X+(I-J_1J_2-J^2_1-J^2_2)g^{-1}(\eta)\right)+\left(g(X)+J^*_2(\eta)\right),\end{equation} by using the properties (i), (iii), (iv), we get: \begin{equation}\hat J^2(X+\eta) =\left(J_1^2X+(I-J_1J_2-J^2_1-J^2_2)J_1g^{-1}(\eta)+ (I-J_1J_2-J^2_1-J^2_2)(X+J_2g^{-1}(\eta))\right) +\left(g(J_1X)+ (I-J_1J_2-J^2_1-J^2_2)^*(\eta)+g(J_2X)+(J_2^*)^2(\eta)\right) =\left((I-J_1J_2-J^2_2)X+(J_1+J_2-2J_1^2J_2-2J^2_2J_1-J_1^3-J_2^3)g^{-1}(\eta)\right) +\left(g(J_1X)+ (I-J_1J_2-J^2_1)^*(\eta)+g(J_2X)\right);\end{equation} moreover, \begin{equation}\hat J^3(X+\eta) =\left((J_1+ (J_1+J_2)-(J_1+J_2)^3)X+(I-J_1J_2-J_1^2-J_2^2)g^{-1}(\eta)\right) +\left(g(X)+(J_2^*+(J_1^*+J_2^*)-(J_1^*+J_2^*)^3(\eta)\right),\end{equation} and the proof is complete.
elements.sent-2411-068: From the proof of the previous proposition, we get that, if $(J_1+J_2)$ is an almost plastic structure on $M$, then, the generalized structure $\hat J$ defined by (\ref{m15 ) satisfies the condition $\hat J^3-\hat J+I=0$.}
elements.sent-2411-069: Therefore, this construction of a generalized structure defines a {\bf duality between the two equations $x^3-x-1=0$ and $x^3-x+1=0$.}
elements.sent-2411-070: Let $\hat J$ be the generalized almost plastic structure defined by (\ref {m15 ).}

elements.section-2411-004: Integrability of some generalized almost plastic structures
elements.sent-2411-074: Let $M$ be a smooth manifold and let $\nabla$ be an affine connection on $M$.
elements.sent-2411-076: We shall study the integrability of some generalized almost plastic structures, characterizing it also in terms of quasi-statistical structures.
elements.sent-2411-079: If $(g,\nabla)$ is a quasi-statistical structure on $M$, then, $(M,g,\nabla)$ is called a quasi-statistical manifold.
elements.subsection-2411-006: Integrability of generalized almost plastic structures induced by two almost plastic structures on $M$
